reason: All 24 FRs mapped to >=1 component (Section 6, confirmed by heatmap in Section 12). All 9 Phase 2 NFRs (NFR-07–NFR-15) addressed with dedicated quality scenarios in Section 7. Module dependency graph (Section 8) shows no true circular dependencies at module level — knowledge→pipeline is correct (HybridKnowledgeV2 uses PromptInjectionDefense + GroundingChecker), and pipeline modules do not actually depend on knowledge despite the SAB block listing it as allowed. Data flow diagrams (Sections 5.1–5.3) are internally consistent and match the pipeline order in Section 2.2. Every Phase 2 FR (FR-14–FR-24) has a complete module specification with responsibility, interface, dependencies, persistence, and logical constraints.
gaps: Minor: SAB block lists 'pipeline' as allowed dependency of 'knowledge' AND 'knowledge' as allowed dependency of 'pipeline' (circular at layer level), but actual module graph shows only knowledge→pipeline, not pipeline→knowledge — allowed_dependencies should match actual dependency graph.

Minor: Section 1.1 lists GroundingChecker L5 in the main pipeline sequence alongside sanitizer/injection-defense/PII-masker, but GroundingChecker operates on LLM outputs (Layer 3), not on user messages — the data flow diagrams (5.1 vs 5.3) handle this correctly but the overview text could mislead implementers into placing it in the wrong pipeline position.
